auto_merge.commit_message:
Specifically for heaptypes that inherit from non-heaptypes we weren't
reference-counting. This is most likely from builtin types, but could
also be from extension types defined in other modules (although it'd be
odd to be mixing heaptypes and non-heaptypes, it isn't forbidden).
auto_merge.commit_title: Fix missed refcounting of heaptype types (#7483)

repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body: Specifically for heaptypes that inherit from non-heaptypes we weren't reference-counting. This is most likely from builtin types, but could also be from extension types defined in other modules (although it'd be odd to be mixing heaptypes and non-heaptypes, it isn't forbidden).

comment_data.body: This was the only class here that I believe was broken. The others are tested just for a bit more completeness
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@
+# mode: run
+
+import sys
+import platform
+
+# For heaptypes the type of an instance is reference counted
+# and creating and destroying an instance should leave the reference count
+# unchanged.
+# For static types the type of an instance isn't reference counted
+# so the test also works (but is less important because it's not our problem).
+
+cdef class C:
+    pass
+
+cdef class D(C):
+    pass
+
+class E(C):
+    pass
+
+cdef class L(list):
+    pass

comment_data.body: Keeping only a borrowed reference to the type is probably ok since we know that it's owned by the instance that we read it from. But it seems at least worth a comment that we thought of it. It's not obvious given the intermediate `tp_dealloc()` upcalls.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1931,6 +1931,10 @@ def generate_dealloc_function(self, scope, code):
             if not base_type.is_builtin_type:
                 base_cname = code.name_in_slot_module_state(base_cname)
             tp_dealloc = TypeSlots.get_base_slot_function(scope, tp_slot)
+            if tp_dealloc is None:
+                code.putln("#if CYTHON_USE_TYPE_SPECS")

commit.message:
Fix missed refcounting of heaptype types

Specifically for heaptypes that inherit from non-heaptypes
we weren't reference-counting. This is most likely from builtin
types, but could also be from extension types defined in other
modules (although it'd be odd to be mixing heaptypes and
non-heaptypes, it isn't forbidden).
